--- title: "Unemployment" editor: visual --- # Data Prep The unemployment rate is from MN DEED LAUS website and includes the average, three year rolling average unemployment rate from 2008 to 2024. I'm going import the entire dataset.
::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} unemployment.original <- read_csv('/Users/kellyasche/Library/CloudStorage/GoogleDrive-kasche@ruralmn.org/My Drive/Research/FY25-27/FY26/OHE Journey to meaningful employment/Data/Unemployment/Master-avg-unemp-rate-county.csv') kable(head(unemployment.original)) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} |countyfp | hs.grad.year| avg.unemp.rate| |:--------|------------:|--------------:| |005 | 2008| 5.866667| |017 | 2008| 5.866667| |073 | 2008| 4.200000| |041 | 2008| 4.400000| |031 | 2008| 4.933333| |059 | 2008| 5.933333| ::: ```{.r .cell-code} kable(names(unemployment.original)) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} |x | |:--------------| |countyfp | |hs.grad.year | |avg.unemp.rate | ::: :::
The dataset above contains each county along with their three year rolling average of unemployment rate. Now, we need to join it up with the master dataset by high school graduation year and county of high school.
::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} master.11 <- read_csv("Data/SLEDS/Masters/Master-11.csv") master.12 <- master.11 %>% left_join(unemployment.original, by = c("hs.grad.year", "countyfp")) unemployment.master <- read_csv("Data/SLEDS/Masters/Master-1.csv") %>% select(PersonID, grad.year, countyfp) %>% left_join(unemployment.original, by = c("grad.year" = "hs.grad.year", "countyfp")) %>% select(-countyfp, -grad.year) write_csv(unemployment.master, "Data/SLEDS/Masters/unemployment_master.csv") kable(head(master.12)) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} | PersonID| After joining with master dataset, we have 65,650 rows along with 104 columns.
# Summary Not really much to summarize here. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} write_csv(master.12, "Data/SLEDS/Masters/Master-12.csv") ``` :::
